Each paragraph must include controlled sentence-length burstiness rather than a flat medium-length distribution.
Sentence-length variance must stay above the configured lower bound for the active cadence profile.
Lexical entropy per sentence must remain within corpus-relative min/max thresholds.
Editing passes may not reduce entropy and idiosyncrasy below the configured preservation floor.
Maintain a rolling paragraph buffer for sentence lengths and trigger burst modulation when medium-length clustering persists.
Compute per-sentence lexical entropy and bias generation toward context-appropriate synonymy when entropy is too low.
Update a narrative-pressure scalar through each scene and bind cadence decisions to that scalar.

source_findings.raw_text: Voice preservation and style drift metrics MUST be applied before and after any automated editing or post-processing step.
For editing pipelines, measure voice preservation and semantic fidelity before enabling new editors in production.
Style drift detector flags intentional stylistic shifts (e.g., POV changes) as regressions.
Editing pipelines slowly erode voice through cumulative small changes that evade single-step checks.
Voice preservation after editing – measure voice_preservation_after_edit_v1 and semantic fidelity on edited_pairs_voice_labelled_v1; compare to human labels.
Integrated pipeline – run full narrative generation, editing, and evaluation chain on mixed benchmark fixtures; ensure no critical validators fail and all scores meet release criteria.

Editing passes may not reduce entropy and idiosyncrasy below the configured preservation floor.
Style-preservation edits must restore voice markers when cleanup operations over-normalize prose.
After each edit pass, compare entropy and idiosyncrasy against the pre-edit reference and restore voice markers when needed.
Style averaging during editing that erodes voice.
Run edit passes that normalize voice and verify post-edit entropy/idiosyncrasy preservation checks catch degradation.

PromptRobustnessValidator

Human vs LLM prose naturalness ranking tasks using parallel prompts with concreteness and texture discriminators.
Prompt paraphrase robustness suites measuring output stability across equivalent prompts.
prompt_sensitivity_failure
robustness_metric
Prompt sensitivity and cross-run stability MUST be measured explicitly for all judge and scoring subsystems.
Template echo detection MUST run on any output produced under few-shot or example-based prompting regimes.
For any change to prompts, rubrics, or fixtures, record a new version identifier and run prompt sensitivity and stability checks.
Apply template echo detector to sample batches for each major prompt or fine-tune version, adjusting thresholds using benchmark fixtures.
Use regression tests to block deployments where stability improves but agreement with human or reference metrics degrades.
LLM-as-judge exhibits prompt sensitivity, self-preference, and rubric drift, masking true quality.
Stability metrics report "stable but wrong" patterns where outputs are consistent yet misaligned with references or human judgment.
Prompt sensitivity – apply prompt_sensitivity_metric_v2 to prompt_variant_family_suite_v1 for all evaluators; ensure sensitivity scores below ceiling per task.

source_findings.raw_text: Every detected failure must be logged to an append-only structured failure ledger.
Only validated failures may be promoted into benchmark fixtures.
Benchmark promotion requires human approval before a fixture becomes part of replayable suites.
Evaluator prompts, thresholds, and model versions must be frozen for any baseline used in promotion decisions.
System changes must pass sandbox evaluation before shadow evaluation.
No change may be promoted to production without benchmark replay against the relevant fixture set.
Human approval is mandatory for rule acceptance, benchmark promotion, and override of critical regressions.
Reflexion-style self-reflection may run only in sandboxed improvement loops, never as a live production writer or updater.
Promotion decisions must reject Pareto-unsafe changes that improve one dimension while dropping another below minimum thresholds.
External dependency changes, including evaluator or model-version changes, require fresh benchmark replay before promotion.
Write every failure event to `failure_log.jsonl` with fixture id, pipeline stage, version metadata, evaluator outputs, and resolution state.
Cluster failure-ledger entries periodically to identify systemic spikes by fixture, genre, style, failure type, and model version.
Convert approved representative failures into versioned benchmark fixtures and record failure-to-fixture lineage.
Run multi-metric regression suites on frozen fixtures before any architecture, prompt, rule, or model change is promoted.
Generate `system_change_proposal` artifacts for every candidate update and route them through sandbox then shadow evaluation.
Use rule-based checks in production runtime; confine LLM-as-judge and Reflexion-style loops to sandbox analysis unless explicitly frozen and approved.

proposals.expected_impact: Defines future fixture families and adversarial scenarios for regression protection and comparative testing.
